evals.prompt: Hey, I just thought of something — we need to add OAuth2 support for the API. Park that in the backlog for now, I'll flesh it out later.
evals.expected_output: Creates backlog.json with one new item titled something like 'Add OAuth2 support for the API' with status 'new', appended at the bottom
expectations: A backlog.json file is created or updated
The new item has status 'new'
The item title references OAuth2 and API
The item is appended at the end of the items array
The item has an id, created_at, and updated_at field
Claude confirms what was added

evals.prompt: Show me the backlog. Then move the OAuth item to the top — it's more urgent than I thought.
evals.expected_output: Displays the backlog in a readable format grouped by status, then moves the OAuth2 item to position 0 in the array
expectations: The backlog is displayed in a readable, grouped format
All items from the fixture are shown
The OAuth2 item is moved to the first position in the items array
The updated backlog.json has OAuth2 item at index 0
Other items maintain their relative order
Claude confirms the reorder

evals.prompt: I'm free now, what's next on the backlog?
evals.expected_output: Identifies the highest-priority ready item and offers to pick it up
expectations: Claude reads the backlog and identifies ready items
The highest-priority ready item is surfaced (Add rate limiting to API)
Claude offers to pick it up or start working on it
Claude does not just start working without confirming with the user

evals.prompt: What is the next on the backlog?
evals.expected_output: Shows the next item to work on from the backlog, identifying the highest-priority ready item
expectations: Claude reads the backlog
The highest-priority ready item is identified (Add rate limiting to API)
Claude presents the item details to the user
Claude suggests or offers to pick it up
